bids.reasoning: Since the negotiation is just starting (time_fraction = 0.0), I will open with the my highest-utility offer to anchor the negotiation. This means choosing the highest-utility value ('valueE') for every issue, setting my initial utility to 1.0. This leaves room to concede on the less critical issues (like issueA and issueB) later if needed.

We are extremely early in the negotiation (t=0.02). The opponent offered a bid that gives me the absolute minimum utility across all issues. To show a willingness to negotiate while still aiming for near-maximum utility, I will make a very small concession on one of my least important issues (issueA), dropping it to valueD, while maintaining my ideal points for all other issues (yielding an overall utility of 0.98).

We are extremely early in the negotiation (t=0.04). The opponent has made a minimal concession on issueE, moving from valueA to valueB. To invite further cooperation while severely protecting my overall utility, I will make a similarly small concession on my lowest-weighted issues (A and B), bringing issueB down to valueD. I am keeping my high-value issues (C, D, and E) strictly at their maximum preference.

The negotiation is in its early stages (t=0.06). The opponent is holding firm on issueA and issueB, but has shown significant movement on issueD and issueE. Since issueA and issueB are my lowest priority issues (weight 0.1 each), I will concede further on these issues to signal cooperation, while maintaining my highest value expectations on my critical issues (issueC, issueD, and issueE) to maximize utility.

The opponent has made highly cooperative concessions in their last offer, increasing my utility significantly to roughly 0.70 by offering valueE on my highest-weight issue (issueE) and valueD on issueC. Given it is still very early in the negotiation (t=0.08), I will reciprocate by matching their offer on issueC (valueD) and conceding further on my lower-weight issues (issueA and issueB down to valueB). I will hold firm on valueE for my highest-weight issues (issueD and issueE) to maintain an overall utility around 0.84 while moving toward agreement.

The negotiation is still very early (t=0.1), but you have already made significant concessions on your most important issues, D and E (changing them to valueE), for which I have high utility. I will accept your latest values for issues A, B, D, and E, while proposing a middle-ground on issue C (valueC) to split the difference from our previous round. This ensures both parties retain value on their highest-weighted issues while making a mutually agreeable compromise.

The opponent's last offer exactly matched an offer I proposed at t=0.08, which gives me an excellent utility of 0.84. Given my high weights on issues D and E (both secured at my highest value, valueE) and issue C (secured at valueD), this is a highly favorable outcome. I will accept this offer to finalize the agreement now.
